chapters.title: cold open
chapters.scriptText:
In the early hours of March 11, 2026, an employee at Stryker Corporation's Irish hub woke up to a ghost in their machine. They didn't see a notification or an incoming call. They saw a factory reset in progress.
Before they were fully awake, it was done. The phone: silent. The laptop in the next room: already dark, its fan winding down to nothing. Not crashed. Not frozen. Gone.
And simultaneously — across nearly eighty countries, on surgical tablets in Michigan, on administrator consoles in Singapore, on the personal phones of thousands of employees who never thought twice about connecting them to the company network — two hundred thousand devices were wiping themselves. A decade of work. Plans, correspondence, secrets — disappearing in real time.
No ransom note. No countdown timer. Just silence, spreading from device to device across the globe, as if a switch had been thrown somewhere in the dark.
A twenty-five billion dollar company had just been erased.
This is the story of the most surgical corporate cyberattack ever documented. A wiper attack conducted entirely without a wiper. And it begins not with a line of malicious code, but with a stolen key — and a door that should never have opened from the outside.
This story is based on the factual reporting of Krebs on Security. Full sources and technical documentation are available in the video description.

scenes.script: In the early hours of March 11, 2026, an employee at Stryker Corporation's Irish hub woke up to a ghost in their machine.
scenes.object_link: The bedroom represents the personal vulnerability of the employee.
scenes.prompt: Photorealistic bedroom interior at dawn, soft blue light filtering through curtains, a smartphone resting on a nightstand with a faint, ominous glow on the screen, cinematic depth of field, high detail, 8k.
scenes.motion: Slow dolly forward toward the nightstand, camera drifts slightly left.

scenes.script: They didn't see a notification or an incoming call. They saw a factory reset in progress. Before they were fully awake, it was done. The phone: silent.
scenes.object_link: The phone represents the primary point of infection.
scenes.prompt: Macro shot of a smartphone screen displaying a progress bar for a factory reset, dark background, sharp focus on the screen, cold blue light, high contrast, cinematic atmosphere.
scenes.motion: Static shot, subtle flickering of the screen light.

scenes.script: The laptop in the next room: already dark, its fan winding down to nothing. Not crashed. Not frozen. Gone.
scenes.object_link: The laptop represents the secondary target of the attack.
scenes.prompt: A dark home office with a laptop on a desk, screen black, faint dust motes in the air, cold ambient lighting, cinematic composition, high detail.
scenes.motion: Slow pan across the desk, camera stops on the dark laptop screen.

scenes.script: And simultaneously — across nearly eighty countries, on surgical tablets in Michigan, on administrator consoles in Singapore, on the personal phones of thousands of employees who never thought twice about connecting them to the company network — two hundred thousand devices were wiping themselves.
scenes.reasoning: Visualizes the massive scale of the device wipe across the globe.

scenes.script: A decade of work. Plans, correspondence, secrets — disappearing in real time. No ransom note. No countdown timer.
scenes.reasoning: Represents the loss of sensitive data and the lack of traditional extortion.

scenes.script: Just silence, spreading from device to device across the globe, as if a switch had been thrown somewhere in the dark.
scenes.object_link: The digital space represents the global network.
scenes.prompt: Abstract digital representation of a global network, nodes going dark one by one in a wave, dark background, glowing blue lines fading to black, cinematic, high tech.
scenes.motion: Slow zoom out from a central point, showing the wave of darkness spreading.

scenes.script: A twenty-five billion dollar company had just been erased.
scenes.reasoning: Highlights the massive financial impact of the attack.
scenes.code:
import React from 'react' import { useCurrentFrame, interpolate } from 'remotion' export const AnimationComponent = () => { const frame = useCurrentFrame() const titleOp = interpolate(frame, [0, 20], [0, 1], { extrapolateLeft: 'clamp', extrapolateRight: 'clamp' }) const titleTy = interpolate(frame, [0, 20], [20, 0], { extrapolateLeft: 'clamp', extrapolateRight: 'clamp' }) const barW = interpolate(frame, [15, 45], [0, 1920], { extrapolateLeft: 'clamp', extrapolateRight: 'clamp' }) // Count up number const countProgress = interpolate(frame, [20, 80], [0, 1], { extrapolateLeft: 'clamp', extrapolateRight: 'clamp' }) const labelOp = interpolate(frame, [25, 40], [0, 1], { extrapolateLeft: 'clamp', extrapolateRight: 'clamp' }) const labelTy = interpolate(frame, [25, 40], [20, 0], { extrapolateLeft: 'clamp', extrapolateRight: 'clamp' }) const subOp = interpolate(frame, [70, 85], [0, 1], { extrapolateLeft: 'clamp', extrapolateRight: 'clamp' }) const subTy = interpolate(frame, [70, 85], [16, 0], { extrapolateLeft: 'clamp', extrapolateRight: 'clamp' }) const divW = interpolate(frame, [30, 55], [0, 300], { extrapolateLeft: 'clamp', extrapolateRight: 'clamp' }) const accentOp = interpolate(frame, [78, 92], [0, 1], { extrapolateLeft: 'clamp', extrapolateRight: 'clamp' }) const title = "TOTAL LOSS" const countValue = "$25B" const countLabel = "MARKET VALUE" const subLabel = "ERASED" const contextText = "STRYKER CORPORATION" // Parse the count value for animation — default 247 for stub const targetNumber = 247 const currentNumber = Math.floor(countProgress * targetNumber) return (
{/* Title */}
{title}
{/* Count label above number */}
{countLabel}
{/* Divider */}
{/* Animated count number */}
{currentNumber}
{/* Static count value shown after animation */}
{countValue}
{/* Sub label */}
{subLabel}
{/* Context text */}
{contextText}
) } export default AnimationComponent

scenes.script: This is the story of the most surgical corporate cyberattack ever documented. A wiper attack conducted entirely without a wiper.
scenes.reasoning: Introduces the core subject of the documentary.

scenes.script: And it begins not with a line of malicious code, but with a stolen key — and a door that should never have opened from the outside.
scenes.reasoning: Visualizes the initial entry point of the attack.

scenes.script: This story is based on the factual reporting of Krebs on Security. Full sources and technical documentation are available in the video description.
scenes.reasoning: Provides source attribution for the documentary.
scenes.code:
import React from 'react' import { useCurrentFrame, interpolate } from 'remotion' export const AnimationComponent = () => { const frame = useCurrentFrame() const bgOp = interpolate(frame, [0, 18], [0, 1], { extrapolateLeft: 'clamp', extrapolateRight: 'clamp' }) const barW = interpolate(frame, [10, 40], [0, 1920], { extrapolateLeft: 'clamp', extrapolateRight: 'clamp' }) const panelOp = interpolate(frame, [12, 28], [0, 1], { extrapolateLeft: 'clamp', extrapolateRight: 'clamp' }) const panelTy = interpolate(frame, [12, 28], [30, 0], { extrapolateLeft: 'clamp', extrapolateRight: 'clamp' }) const tagOp = interpolate(frame, [22, 36], [0, 1], { extrapolateLeft: 'clamp', extrapolateRight: 'clamp' }) const titleOp = interpolate(frame, [28, 44], [0, 1], { extrapolateLeft: 'clamp', extrapolateRight: 'clamp' }) const titleTy = interpolate(frame, [28, 44], [20, 0], { extrapolateLeft: 'clamp', extrapolateRight: 'clamp' }) const divW = interpolate(frame, [38, 60], [0, 800], { extrapolateLeft: 'clamp', extrapolateRight: 'clamp' }) const bodyOp = interpolate(frame, [48, 65], [0, 1], { extrapolateLeft: 'clamp', extrapolateRight: 'clamp' }) const bodyTy = interpolate(frame, [48, 65], [16, 0], { extrapolateLeft: 'clamp', extrapolateRight: 'clamp' }) const metaOp = interpolate(frame, [62, 78], [0, 1], { extrapolateLeft: 'clamp', extrapolateRight: 'clamp' }) const stampOp = interpolate(frame, [72, 88], [0, 1], { extrapolateLeft: 'clamp', extrapolateRight: 'clamp' }) const stampScale = interpolate(frame, [72, 88], [1.4, 1], { extrapolateLeft: 'clamp', extrapolateRight: 'clamp' }) const title = "REPORTING SOURCE" const label1 = "KREBS ON SECURITY" const label2 = "FACTUAL REPORTING" const label3 = "TECHNICAL DOCS" const tag1 = "VERIFIED" const tag2 = "PUBLIC" const contextText = "SOURCES IN DESCRIPTION" const alertText = "DOCUMENTATION" return (
{/* Top accent */}
{/* Bottom bar */}
{/* Main evidence panel */}
{/* Header bar */}
{title}
{/* Tags row */}
{tag1}
{tag2}
{/* Evidence title */}
{label1}
{/* Divider */}
{/* Body text */}
{contextText}
{/* Meta row */}
{label2} {label3}
{/* Bottom alert bar */}
{alertText}
{/* Stamp — right side */}
{tag1}
) } export default AnimationComponent
